stabilityRule: IDs are immutable once published. Change a title or proof in place; add a new ID for a distinct obligation; never reuse or renumber a retired ID.

ownerVocabulary.default: Values in owners name target-architecture components from §04.

exceptions.Native supply chain: WP1 · Native supply chain from §28; §04 defines no build or release owner.

tests.title: Real vendor TUI fidelity
tests.proof: Replay the canonical provider VT corpus through the headless host and attached surface and compare cells, cursor, modes, effects, and frames with the uninterrupted real TUI and standalone Ghostty reference.

tests.title: User-known live terminal
tests.proof: Close pane/window, quit, crash, and expire visibility for exact generations; require positive PTY, host, and process-tree absence or explicit survivors/unknown.

tests.title: Single PTY writer
tests.proof: Instrument every PTY write during mixed human, automated, renderer-effect, reconnect, and fault traffic; require one host arbiter and one total byte order.

tests.title: Human draft priority
tests.proof: Inject typing, paste, IME, mouse, automated transactions, disconnects, and lease expiry at every claim boundary; automation must never enter while the human claim is held or uncertain.

tests.title: Exact locator addressing
tests.proof: Exercise display-name reuse, stale generation, foreign instance, and tagged root/agent subjects; only the exact locator may operate and all stale targets return typed errors.

tests.title: Structured sourced status
tests.proof: Run duplicate, gap, conflict, spoof, freshness, and reducer-prefix fixtures; every visible field retains source, observed time, revision, freshness, and confidence and terminal text remains diagnostic only.

tests.title: No focus steal
tests.proof: Emit output, status, message, attention, attach, and recovery events into key and non-key windows; first responder changes only after explicit human navigation.

tests.title: Daemon-owned authority
tests.proof: Attempt delivery across denied sender, recipient, instance, edge, capability epoch, and gate combinations; agents and terminal channels cannot create an authoritative transition.

tests.title: Self-contained universal macOS release
tests.proof: Install quarantined artifacts on supported Intel and Apple Silicon Macs with tmux, Ghostty, Zig, shell plugins, package managers, and network absent; verify inventory, signatures, notarization, and launch.

tests.title: Bounded terminal resources
tests.proof: Run the 19-session soak and renderer-churn corpus; enforce visibility admission plus measured journal, checkpoint, image, scrollback, client-queue, RSS, disk, CPU, and GPU caps.

tests.title: Manual-I/O bridge
tests.proof: Build the exact §23 C ABI against the pinned commit, verify one header/six implementation files/1,500 non-test lines, and pass key, text, IME, mouse, output, reconnect, header, and symbol ABI tests.

tests.title: Complete checkpoint
tests.proof: At every adversarial byte boundary restore both screens and all §23 opaque state; restored digest and subsequent effects must equal uninterrupted execution.

tests.title: Renderer resource budget
tests.proof: Measure the current supported attached-pane profile and 19 visible hosts on supported Intel and Apple Silicon against every §28 CPU, memory, GPU, latency, and disk budget. The future one-selected-plus-two-warm profile is measured and recorded separately as nonblocking Split Horizon input.

tests.title: Provider delivery evidence
tests.proof: For every supported Claude, Codex, and Grok build verify §25 readiness and receipt behavior under idle, busy, approval, modal, backpressure, crash, and 1 MiB input without timer or screen-text substitution.

tests.title: Crash isolation and visibility lease
tests.proof: Kill broker, Workspace, and individual hosts; re-adopt only exact visible generations with complete identity evidence, expire invisible generations, and prove one-host blast radius.

tests.title: Process containment
tests.proof: Exercise provider roots, double-fork, setsid, app servers, recovery children, PID reuse, and unrelated sentinels; require verified absence or explicit survivors for every captured identity.

tests.title: Legacy deletion
tests.proof: After two stable releases prove zero live legacy generations, no compatibility writes/direct calls, clean offline install with tmux absent, and successful admission rollback before deleting readers and flags.

tests.title: First correct frame latency
tests.proof: Across 500 randomized attaches/retargets in the 19-session corpus, require target frame digest before timing ends and p95 at most 150 ms warm and 500 ms cold.

tests.title: Human input acceptance latency
tests.proof: Timestamp 10,000 local key, text, IME, paste, and mouse events; Hive overhead to PTY-write-queue acceptance must be p95 at most 8 ms and p99 at most 16 ms.

tests.title: Visible status latency
tests.proof: Run 10,000 live-source events through the flat reducer while UI is visible; visible projection must be p95 at most 250 ms and p99 at most 500 ms.

tests.title: Lossless ordered exact-target I/O
tests.proof: Digest and sequence-check 100 MiB output per session and 1 MiB transactions with reconnect/crash at every chunk boundary; require zero loss, duplication, reorder, or wrong target.

tests.title: Crash adoption and teardown latency
tests.proof: Across 1,000 injected failures require visible-host re-adoption p95 at most 2 s and zero owned providers after the 15 s visibility lease plus 4 s graceful-stop bound.

tests.title: Idle host resource budget
tests.proof: After warmup measure five-minute steady-state medians on supported Intel and Apple Silicon: 19 hosts plus broker CPU at most 1%, broker RSS at most 64 MiB, fresh host overhead at most 24 MiB.

tests.title: Attached renderer memory budget
tests.proof: Measure the current attached-pane profile on representative TUIs, screens, images, and scrollback; total terminal infrastructure for 19 sessions must be at most 1.5 GiB with per-session caps. The future one-selected-plus-two-warm profile is measured and recorded separately as nonblocking Split Horizon input.

tests.title: Visible refresh and detached GPU quiescence
tests.proof: Use Instruments during high output and rapid retarget: visible interaction sustains display refresh/60 fps where supported, and detached or destroyed surfaces submit no display-link or render work.

tests.title: Bounded terminal storage
tests.proof: Run 19 sessions for eight hours with scrollback, images, checkpoints, and forced failures; storage remains at most 2 GiB per HIVE_HOME with bounded journals and exactly two checkpoints.

tests.title: No authority, truth, focus, or supply-chain violation
tests.proof: Run security, UI, provider, Gatekeeper, offline, capability-epoch, cross-instance, and hostile-sequence suites; require no focus steal, unauthorized observe/input/kill, false receipt/state, or unsigned/runtime-fetched code.

tests.title: Ghostty fidelity suite
tests.proof: Run VT goldens and real Claude/Codex/Grok login, tool, approval, alternate-screen, resize, scrollback, search, image, and synchronized-output workflows against standalone Ghostty.

tests.title: Input suite
tests.proof: Cover layouts, Option/control/function keys, IME, 1-byte through 1-MiB paste, bracketed paste, mouse, slow PTY, claims/disconnects, and all message priorities without loss, reorder, interleave, wrong target, logging, or premature release.

tests.title: Crash and recovery suite
tests.proof: Kill each Workspace, renderer, client, daemon, broker, host, provider root, and app server during every lifecycle/input/output state and verify lease, isolation, reattach, receipt, lock, and survivor truth.

tests.title: Identity and security suite
tests.proof: Exercise PID and display-name reuse, stale/foreign identity, socket/token attacks, epoch/gate denial, hostile escape sequences, OSC 52, hyperlinks, and oversized images/frames; fail every unauthorized or unbounded operation.

tests.title: Status suite
tests.proof: Cover out-of-order, duplicate, missing, stale, conflicting, disconnected, spoofed, generation-changed, snapshot-gap, and cross-language reducer cases without self-approval or invented freshness/work.

tests.title: Scale and performance suite
tests.proof: Exercise 19+ sessions, transient reviewers, output bursts, selection churn, idle, images, scrollback, sleep/wake, display changes, renderer churn, and eight-hour soak without backpressure loss, unbounded resources, lag, or hot off-screen rendering.

tests.title: Release suite
tests.proof: On supported clean Intel and Apple Silicon macOS versions test install, upgrade, rollback, quarantine, Gatekeeper, notarization, offline launch, missing tmux/Ghostty/Zig, architecture compatibility, and preservation of live generations.

tests.title: Instance-scoped session target
tests.proof: Foreign-instance and invalid session targets are rejected before operation; exact instance/subject/generation locators are required.

tests.title: Session existence discovery
tests.proof: Exact live generation returns present, missing service/generation returns absent, and all other discovery failures remain typed errors/unknown.

tests.title: Detached session creation
tests.proof: Create exactly once in the requested worktree with bounded geometry/profile and verify locator, process/start token, PTY, executable, process group, engine, and socket before returning created.

tests.title: Launch failure remains observable
tests.proof: A provider launch failure yields structured exit/readiness evidence and a bounded diagnostic path before cleanup; absence of a terminal screen never erases the cause.

tests.title: Literal ordered message input
tests.proof: Send binary-safe text plus submit as one authorized transaction to the exact generation under slow PTY and faults; verify bytes once, in order, with honest receipt.

tests.title: Interrupt clears stale composer before control
tests.proof: Urgent/critical cancellation revokes the in-flight turn, clears the old composer through the provider adapter, and admits control only after ordered evidence; cancelled work cannot resume.

tests.title: Bounded terminal observation
tests.proof: Authorized inspect/snapshot returns bounded terminal observation for diagnostics and reconnect while status and delivery truth remain structured rather than inferred from screen text.

tests.title: Attached-user protection
tests.proof: A user-visible attached generation is represented by a current Workspace lease and cannot be replaced or terminated as stale; detached transport alone grants no lifetime.

tests.title: Provider process-root discovery
tests.proof: Inspect returns verified root and auxiliary PID/start-token identities before signaling and attributes their resource use to one exact generation.

tests.title: Exact-generation verified termination
tests.proof: Close/kill revokes input, captures roots, stops the exact generation, closes PTY/host, and requires wait/absence evidence for every identity or returns survivors/unknown.

tests.title: Instance generation enumeration
tests.proof: List returns only the canonical HIVE_HOME/instance generation records that live hosts re-prove; missing service is empty and corrupt/unverifiable records are quarantined as unknown.

tests.title: Persistent real orchestrator TUI launch
tests.proof: Launch the selected root provider once in the requested directory/profile, show its real TUI, and preserve it across renderer detachment only while visibility remains current.

tests.title: Workspace attach waits for exact live target
tests.proof: An attach grant binds the exact locator; the surface stays in an honest attaching state until verified checkpoint/journal/live output reaches the target high-water and first correct frame.

tests.title: Renderer replacement does not kill a visible generation
tests.proof: Renderer/transport detach or retarget leaves the exact process alive only under a current visibility lease; user Close follows termination and never this detach path.

tests.title: Bounded scroll and mouse routing
tests.proof: Wheel gestures reach the provider's active mouse protocol at correct cells or bounded native scrollback without unbounded subprocess/gesture queues or false button events.

tests.title: Bounded scrollback history
tests.proof: Long output retains the configured bounded scrollback/checkpoint/journal history needed for display and search without exceeding per-session and HIVE_HOME caps.

tests.title: Same-generation reconnect
tests.proof: Broker/renderer reconnect proves the same locator, process/start token, build, socket, secret, visibility, and output high-water; it never substitutes a new process or partial screen.

tests.title: Authentic terminal rendering and modes
tests.proof: Render real provider primary/alternate screens, Unicode/graphemes, colors, cursor, hyperlinks, keyboard/mouse modes, images, and synchronized output against the canonical corpus and standalone Ghostty.

tests.title: Child starts with settled cell geometry
tests.proof: Defer create/attach until nonzero settled pane geometry, apply one canonical cell/pixel size, and require the first frame and PTY size to agree.

tests.title: Live resize and reflow
tests.proof: Rapid window/layout/display resizes coalesce to monotonic geometry revisions, update PTY/headless/surface state in order, and preserve correct reflow, cursor, and final frame.

tests.title: Bounded provider terminal environment
tests.proof: Launch receives the Hive terminal profile and required PATH/TMPDIR/worktree values without importing Ghostty configuration or leaking unrelated secrets.

tests.title: Native key and text encoding
tests.proof: macOS layouts, modifiers, control/function keys, text, and terminal keyboard modes encode through Ghostty and enter the claimed exact-generation arbiter once and in order.

tests.title: Claimed bounded paste
tests.proof: Paste from 1 byte through 1 MiB acquires the human claim, honors bracketed-paste mode and clipboard policy, remains exact-target and ordered, and never enters content logs.

tests.title: Native mouse encoding
tests.proof: Clicks, drags, motion, and wheels at pane edges and scaled geometry encode the correct terminal cell/mode under a human claim without wrong-target bytes.

tests.title: No false mouse release
tests.proof: All-motion hover never emits a no-button packet that decodes as release; real clicks, drags, and wheels remain byte-correct.

tests.title: Honest explicit terminal focus
tests.proof: Click and keyboard navigation make the intended surface first responder and align its focus indicator; new panes, output, status, attach, and recovery never steal focus.

tests.title: Surface child cleanup
tests.proof: Destroying a renderer closes its transport resources without killing a still-visible generation; terminal Close separately requests verified exact-generation termination.

tests.title: Terminal exit is surfaced
tests.proof: Host/provider exit publishes structured wait, last-sequence, checkpoint, survivor, and failure evidence to daemon and surface; renderer disappearance alone cannot invent exit.

tests.title: Deterministic visible-grid inspection
tests.proof: Test-only visible-grid and frame digests identify the exact applied high-water and match canonical headless state without becoming production status or receipt evidence.

tests.title: Human composer claim signals
tests.proof: First editing key, paste, IME start, deletion, or edit command acquires/renews the claim; submit/cancel releases only after ordered acknowledgement and selection changes do not retarget composition.

tests.title: Native selection and copy
tests.proof: Mouse/keyboard selection, Select All, and Copy operate on the attached surface's semantic cells and scrollback, obey clipboard policy, and do not mutate focus or terminal bytes.
